refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and duty cycle context to make verification concrete. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 cycle time computed from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target acceptance threshold; safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below CT_target.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time metrics via telemetry interface.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical subsystems for speed; minimize teleoperation latency via low-latency network link; design safety services with non-blocking async checks.
DP-B: Implement predictive cycle time modeling and real-time adaptive control to maintain p95 ≤ CT_target under varying site conditions.
DP-C: Allocate cycle time budget across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems; enforce budget via design reviews and integration testing.
DP-D: Establish telemetry-driven feedback loop to continuously monitor p95 cycle time and trigger corrective actions when threshold is approached.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C + DP-D (integrated): Allocate explicit cycle time budget across vehicle subsystem, teleoperation subsystem, and safety services; enforce via design reviews and integration testing. Establish continuous telemetry-driven monitoring of p95 cycle time with feedback to operations and engineering for corrective action.

potential_sources: Vehicle cycle time is coupled to hydraulic response, load mass, and terrain (site conditions).
Teleoperation latency is coupled to network link quality and tele-operator skill.
Safety services latency is coupled to sensor response time and safety logic complexity.
Telemetry reporting overhead may introduce minor coupling to network bandwidth.
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP decouples the three subsystems via explicit budget allocation and monitoring, reducing cross-subsystem coupling. Telemetry feedback is asynchronous and non-blocking, minimizing impact on cycle time itself. High safety criticality requires that safety services budget is conservative and non-negotiable.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle mechanical/hydraulic limits and site conditions.
Assumption: Network link supports low-latency teleoperation without excessive jitter.
Assumption: Safety services can be designed with bounded, predictable latency.
Risk: If site conditions (e.g., soft terrain, extreme temperature) degrade vehicle performance, CT_target may not be achievable without design revision.
Risk: Teleoperation latency spikes due to network congestion could violate cycle time budget.
Risk: Safety service complexity growth could exceed allocated latency budget.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP to explicitly state cycle time budget allocation percentages (e.g., Vehicle: 70%, Teleoperation: 20%, Safety: 10%) and define contingency margins. Add requirement for network link SLA (latency, jitter, availability) to support teleoperation budget.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module responsible for coordinating vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services subsystems to achieve cycle time KPI. Owns cycle time budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and corrective action orchestration.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits cycle time metrics (load start, travel start, dump start, dump end timestamps; computed p95 cycle time) to external monitoring/analytics systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives duty cycle task definitions (load mass, travel distance, site conditions) from Site Manager or planning system.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts if cycle time optimization attempts conflict with safety constraints or if safety services report latency violations.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution coordinates network link quality monitoring and teleoperation latency feedback to ensure teleoperation budget is maintained.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of cycle time KPI. Cycle time budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and corrective action orchestration are system-level concerns. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry emission and tasking reception cross boundary.

Vehicle owns mechanical/hydraulic cycle time performance but does not own teleoperation latency or safety services latency. Assigning DP-C to Vehicle alone would require Vehicle to coordinate across subsystems, violating separation of concerns.
Teleoperation owns latency control but does not own vehicle mechanical performance or safety services. Assigning DP-C to Teleoperation alone would create artificial coupling.
Safety Services owns safety latency budget but does not own vehicle or teleoperation performance. Assigning DP-C to Safety Services alone would be inappropriate.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Cycle time KPI is a system-level performance metric that requires coordination across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems. NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner because it has visibility and authority over all three subsystems and can enforce budget allocation, monitor telemetry, and trigger corrective actions.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a coordination bottleneck if cycle time optimization requires frequent cross-subsystem negotiation.
Risk: Telemetry aggregation and feedback loop may introduce latency if not carefully designed (asynchronous, non-blocking).
Risk: Corrective action orchestration may require changes to vehicle, teleoperation, or safety subsystems, which could exceed NGHE Solution's authority if subsystems are independently owned.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interfaces and escalation paths between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services for cycle time budget violations. Establish telemetry schema and feedback loop design to ensure monitoring does not introduce latency. Clarify ownership and change authority for cycle time budget reallocation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (budget allocation + telemetry monitoring) directly addresses FR (achieve p95 ≤ CT_target). DP is logically sound: explicit budget allocation decouples subsystems, and continuous monitoring enables corrective action. Assumptions and risks are identified and manageable.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry emission and tasking reception are external interfaces; minimal diffusion.
Cycle time budget allocation and corrective action orchestration are contained within NGHE Solution; no diffusion to Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services at this level.
Risk: If corrective actions require changes to subsystem behavior, diffusion may increase at lower decomposition levels.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner with 95% containment score. Cycle time KPI is a system-level concern that requires top-level coordination. Subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) will own their respective budget allocations at lower decomposition levels.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is measurable via telemetry (timestamps for load, travel, dump events). p95 computation is straightforward statistical analysis. Verification can be performed in field trials under representative duty cycle. Safety criticality requires correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time budget allocation to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services is clear and can be enforced via design reviews and integration testing. Telemetry-driven feedback loop provides continuous visibility and enables corrective action.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP to specify cycle time budget allocation percentages and contingency margins.
Define network link SLA (latency, jitter, availability) to support teleoperation budget.
Establish telemetry schema and feedback loop design to ensure monitoring does not introduce latency.
Define escalation paths and change authority for cycle time budget reallocation.
Add safety criticality tailoring: ensure safety services budget is conservative and non-negotiable; add safety event correlation analysis to verification plan.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-002 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into sub-FRs for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Selected DP (budget allocation + telemetry monitoring) is sound and well-contained in NGHE Solution, but implementation requires lower-level decomposition to allocate budgets and define subsystem-level cycle time requirements. High safety criticality requires explicit safety tailoring at lower levels.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated cycle time budget (e.g., 70% of CT_target).
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget (e.g., 20% of CT_target).
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below CT_target; safety latency budget shall be conservative and non-negotiable (e.g., 10% of CT_target).
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time metrics via telemetry interface; telemetry feedback loop shall not introduce latency exceeding 5% of CT_target.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle mechanical/hydraulic limits and site conditions.
Network link supports low-latency teleoperation without excessive jitter.
Safety services can be designed with bounded, predictable latency.
Cycle time budget allocation percentages (Vehicle: 70%, Teleoperation: 20%, Safety: 10%) are feasible; to be validated in lower-level decomposition.
Telemetry aggregation and feedback loop can be designed asynchronously and non-blocking.
open_risks: Site conditions (soft terrain, extreme temperature) may degrade vehicle performance below CT_target.
Teleoperation latency spikes due to network congestion could violate cycle time budget.
Safety service complexity growth could exceed allocated latency budget.
Corrective action orchestration may require changes to subsystems, which could exceed NGHE Solution's authority.
Telemetry feedback loop may introduce latency if not carefully designed.
evidence_needed: Vehicle performance data (cycle time vs. load mass, terrain, temperature) to validate vehicle budget allocation.
Network link SLA and latency characterization to validate teleoperation budget allocation.
Safety services latency analysis to validate safety budget allocation.
Telemetry schema and feedback loop design review to ensure monitoring does not introduce latency.
Field trial results demonstrating p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target under representative duty cycle.
Safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise from cycle time optimization.
